title: OpenClaw 2026.4.12: Stability, Audio, and Memory Fixes
summary: OpenClaw's latest update focuses on stability, audio transcription, and memory improvements. The release also includes fixes for Telegram approval deadlocks and timezone issues in dreaming.
body:
OpenClaw has released version 2026.4.12, packed with stability and reliability improvements. The update addresses audio transcription issues and enhances chat, TTS, and WhatsApp functionalities. Memory, QMD, plugin, cron, and subagent systems have also been fine-tuned.

This release is significant for users who rely on OpenClaw for seamless AI interactions. The fixes for Telegram approval deadlocks and timezone issues in dreaming highlight the project's commitment to a smooth user experience. Compared to previous versions, this update emphasizes robustness and cross-platform consistency.

The community's reaction to this update is expected to be positive, especially among users who faced issues with audio transcription and Telegram integration. Future updates may focus on expanding plugin capabilities and further refining memory management. The open-source nature of OpenClaw ensures continuous improvements driven by community feedback.
